params.name: George VI's Speech
params.description:
On Aug 20, 1939 AD, this speech was handed over to King George VI.
This speech was used by King George VI of Britain in his mobilization speech during World War II. At that time, George V passed away, and his successor, Edward VIII, abdicated the throne for love. George VI, who suffered from severe stuttering, was pushed to the throne. With the help of a speech therapist Rogge, after undergoing a series of training, he finally overcame his psychological barriers and successfully delivered the …
